summary: A wildly inventive ’78 second set with scorching Jack‑a‑Roe → Playing in the Band → Shakedown Street and a rare World to Give, but the tape is riddled with cuts and missing sections.
review: Fans rave about this show’s second set – calling it jazzy, trippy and one of the best of ’78, highlighted by an insane jam into Jack‑a‑Roe, the Playin’ → Shakedown Street → World to Give sequence and a rare third performance of “If I Had The World To Give.”  The recording, however, is far from pristine; multiple cuts, missing songs and splice artifacts detract from an otherwise legendary performance.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (mostly 4‑5) praise the musical performance while noting significant recording issues; weighting shows heavy emphasis on show quality yields a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Several audible cuts and missing portions (e.g., start of Peggy-O, Lazy/Supplication, early drums), plus splice artifacts; overall listenable but fragmented.

standout_songs: If I Had The World To Give
show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is a continuous, improvisational surge with an unusual jam opener, wild transitions and a jazzy feel; fans describe it as one of the most inspired ’78 shows despite its erratic recording.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Some reviewers note Jerry’s tone sounds more like his early‑'80 style than typical ’78.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mixed comments on Bob’s presence – some say he was sick and missing, others hear him clearly on Shakedown Street backing vocals and rhythm guitar.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboard work is described as wild yet tight, often giving away the band’s direction.
